name: GeoContext Environmental Research Agent
description: Give it a location (coordinates or a place name) and an environmental question; it plans which of 17 authoritative geospatial data sources to query (EPA ECHO, USFWS IPaC/NWI, GBIF, iNaturalist, USGS NLCD/3DEP/NWIS, EPA/USGS Water Quality Portal, USGS PAD-US, NIFC, FEMA NFHL, USDA SSURGO, OpenStreetMap, Open-Meteo, Brave), runs them, synthesizes a cited answer, validates it against the gathered facts, and returns both a human-readable answer and reusable structured data. Never fabricates; reports confidence and limitations.

skills.name: Site environmental profile
skills.description: A multi-source profile for a location: land use, contamination sources, terrain, flood risk, and protected status — cited, with limitations noted.
examples: Give me a full site profile for 5700 Arsenal St, St. Louis: contamination, land use, elevation, and flood risk.

skills.name: Habitat & species assessment
skills.description: Land cover/habitat at a point plus ESA-listed species, critical habitat, migratory birds (USFWS IPaC), and species actually recorded nearby (GBIF/iNaturalist). Ideal for camera-trap and habitat-suitability questions.
examples: What habitat and listed species are at these camera-trap coordinates 44.4237, -110.5885?
Is this area suitable habitat for the Indiana bat near 38.2, -90.7?

skills.name: Contamination & environmental quality
skills.description: EPA-regulated facilities and compliance history (EPA ECHO), nearby water bodies and monitoring (USGS/EPA), and current air quality near a location.
examples: What contamination sources are near Forest Park, St. Louis?

skills.name: Hazards & protected status
skills.description: FEMA flood zone, recorded wildfire history, mapped wetlands, and whether a point is in or near a protected area (parks/refuges/easements, with GAP status).
examples: Is 29.95, -90.07 in a FEMA flood zone, and has this area burned before?
